title: Nanofiltration and microfiltration for the removal of chromium, total dissolved solids, and sulfate from water
author: Zolfaghari, Kargar

steps.title: Analysis method
steps.instruction: The concentrations of chromium (Cr(VI)) were analyzed in the feed solution under different pH conditions with an advanced Atomic Absorption Spectrometer (AAS), Contraa-700 Model. Furthermore, the concentration of sulfate anion (Sigma-Aldrich) in the samples was analyzed with a UV–vis Array Spectrophotometer (Photonix Ar 2015, Teifsanje Pishro Pajohesh Company, Iran). The pH was adjusted using NaOH and HCl. The chemicals used in this study (K2Cr3O7, NaOH, Na2SO4, and HCl) were purchased from Merck Co. with a purity percentage of more than 99.99%. Chromium solution was prepared using distilled water. Many samples from tap water were tested and analyzed as well. Experiments in the laboratory temperature (25 °C) were conducted.
